meaning: n.(=jugglery)欺骗,杂耍adj.欺骗的,欺诈的,变戏法(似)的
examples: He can perform an astonishing variety of acts, including mime and juggling.
Learning memory systems, mental sports and juggling are all wonderful ways to maintain a vibrant, lively mind.
Soon she was juggling five eggs.
Juggling business with child care does not, of course, make for a placid life.
The cooler fad eventually cooled, but the Sands family kept juggling brands and varieties.
